# spark-html

> Spark is a no-build reactive HTML framework: the file you write is the file
> that runs. No compiler, no virtual DOM, no build step for the user. HTML +
> `{expr}` + a handful of directives + stores — one runtime (~16 KB gzip, 0
> deps) that works the same as a plain client app, a build-time-prerendered
> static site, or a full-stack server-rendered app via the spark-ssr sibling
> package (inferred database, REST API, auth, jobs — the HTML template is the
> spec). A page written for one mode runs in the other two by relocation, not
> rewrite.

Core ideas worth knowing before reading further: components are plain `.html`
files (markup + `<script>` + `<style>`, scoped by default); reactivity is
plain `let`/assignment — no signals, no hooks, no dependency arrays; `<spark-ssr>`
blocks declare data (SQL, a table, a URL, a markdown glob, or a JS module) and
the server infers schema, REST endpoints, and hydration from how the template
reads it.

## Template syntax reference

A component is one `.html` file: markup, then an optional `<script>` (top-level
`let`/`const` = reactive state — nothing to import, nothing to call), then an
optional scoped `<style>`.

- Text binding: `<p>Hello {name}</p>`
- Expressions: `{price * qty}`, `{ok ? 'yes' : 'no'}`
- Events: `<button onclick={add}>` — handler receives the DOM event
- Dynamic attributes: `<button :disabled="count >= 10">` — booleans toggle, others set
- Attribute interpolation: `<input value="{draft}">`
- Loops: `<template each="todo in todos">…</template>` (must be on `<template>`
  so it's valid HTML anywhere, including inside `<ul>`/`<table>`)
- Loop with index: `<template each="todo, i in todos">`
- Keyed loops: `<template each="row in rows" key="row.id">` — reuse nodes by
  identity across reorders (add this whenever items reorder)
- Conditional display (element stays, toggles a property): `<div :hidden="!show">`
- Dynamic class: `<li :class="done ? 'item done' : 'item'">`
- Two-way binding: `bind:value` (text/number/`<select>`/contenteditable),
  `bind:checked` (checkbox), `bind:group` (radio)
- Conditional blocks (nodes enter/leave the DOM): `<template if="show">…</template>`,
  chained `<template else-if="…">` / `<template else>` directly after — exactly
  one branch renders, short-circuit like real if/else
- Async blocks: `<template await="loadUser(id)" as="user">…<template then>{user.name}</template>
  <template catch>{user.message}</template></template>` — reactive, re-fires on
  state change and cancels the prior promise; `await="once(expr)"` fires once
- Slots: `<slot></slot>`, `<slot name="title"></slot>` — project caller content
- Escape hatch: `<pre spark-ignore>` — subtree never patched, literal `{braces}`
  survive (also the fix for code samples in markup)
- Literal brace outside spark-ignore: backslash-escape it, `\{`/`\}` (HTML
  entities like `&#123;` do NOT work — the browser decodes them first)
- Reactive statements: `$: total = price * qty` (Svelte-style, re-runs when
  its dependencies change); lifecycle via `onMount(() => { ... return cleanup; })`
- Components: `<div import="/components/card" title="{t}"></div>` — props are
  plain attributes, frozen at mount (not reactive after — pass state that must
  stay live via a store, or inline the UI in the parent instead)
- Stores: `store('name', initialValue)` creates/gets a shared reactive store;
  `useStore('name')` subscribes a component to it; `derived(fn, [stores])` for
  memoized computed values across stores

## spark-ssr — full-stack quick reference

A page is an HTML file under `pages/`. A `<spark-ssr>` block is the entire
backend for that page — no separate server file, no ORM, no migrations:

    <template each="todo in todos">
      <input type="checkbox" bind:checked="todo.done" onchange={toggle}>
      {todo.title}
      <button onclick={remove}>x</button>
    </template>
    <input bind:value="draft"><button onclick={add}>Add</button>
    <spark-ssr table="todos" live />

That one line infers a `todos` table from how the template reads it (columns +
types), creates it, generates `GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE /api/todos`, renders rows
server-side, and hydrates the page. `live` pings every open tab over SSE on
every write. A page's data doesn't have to be a table — name a variable and
point it at SQL (`posts = SELECT * FROM posts WHERE published = 1`), a URL
(server-fetched), a markdown glob (`docs = ./content/*.md` — zero database),
or a JS module (`weather = ./lib/weather.js`, `export default (req, db) => value`).

Any handler your template references but doesn't define is **synthesized**
for you (`onclick={add}` outside a loop → insert; inside a loop next to a
`bind:*` → update; inside a loop alone → delete). Write only the handlers with
custom logic; `<spark-ssr auto="none">` turns synthesis off entirely.

Ambient helpers — in scope on a hydrating page with zero imports:
- `api_create(body)` / `api_update(id, body)` / `api_delete(id)` — REST calls
  to the page's inferred table (pass the table name first if the page has more
  than one: `api_create('posts', {…})`)
- `refresh()` — re-runs every declared source through the per-request JSON
  mirror and reassigns the page's vars; source-agnostic (table/SQL/URL/glob/module)
- `navigate` — wire as `<nav onclick={navigate}>`: intercepts same-origin,
  same-path `<a href="?with=2">` clicks, swaps the URL via `history.pushState`,
  and refetches via `refresh()` instead of a full reload. Cross-page links fall
  through untouched. Also wires `popstate` so back/forward reloads correctly.
  Page-scoped like the other four — an imported `<div import="/components/…">`
  component cannot call it (see Limitations).

Batteries included, declared in markup, not code: auth (email/password
sessions inferred from a `user_id` column; built-in `/login`/`/signup`/`/logout`;
`guard="session"` protects a page; a `user_id` column auto-scopes a table to
its owner); no-JS forms (`<form action="/api/posts" method="post" redirect="/admin">`
works with JavaScript off, 303 back on success, constraint attributes
validated server-side); relations (`each="c in post.comments"` infers a
`comments` table with a `post_id` FK and joins it); jobs & mail
(`<spark-ssr job="digest" every="1d" />` or `on="insert:orders"`, `mail()`
ambient from config); free extras (`sitemap.xml`, `robots.txt`,
`/__spark/openapi.json` + a typed client, all generated from the inferred
backend).

## Limitations (read before assuming a gap is a bug)

- **One scope per component.** All top-level declarations share the component
  scope. JS `import` statements work (transformed to dynamic imports);
  `import.meta` is not available inside component scripts.
- **Block scoping.** Only top-level `let`/`const` become component state.
  Inside a function body they're true block-scoped locals; destructured
  declarations stay local everywhere.
- **Reactivity depth.** Plain objects/arrays are deeply reactive; `Map`/`Set`
  mutations (`set`/`add`/`delete`/`clear`) are tracked too. Class instances and
  `Date` are NOT — reassign the variable to update.
- **Loop reconciliation.** Reuse is positional by default; reordering a list
  without `key` keeps nodes in place and rewrites their contents. Add
  `key="…"` for identity-stable moves.
- **The rewriter is a scanner, not a parser.** String- and comment-aware —
  code-shaped text inside string literals stays byte-intact. The one construct
  it can't parse: a regex literal containing a quote character (warns loudly,
  names the fix — move the regex to an imported `.js` module).
- **Coarse store updates.** Stores are deeply reactive but each notification
  re-renders subscribers with a full (cheap) pass — not tracked per key like
  component-local state. Use `derived()` for memoized computed values.
- **Hydrating SSR pages never run their `<script>` on the server.** On an
  interactive `spark-ssr` page (handlers or binds), the script becomes the
  CLIENT component only. Compute display fields (formatted dates, counts,
  derived text) in the data source (SQL expression or module), not the page
  script, or the first paint won't have them. A non-interactive page keeps the
  escape hatch: its plain script runs server-side. Permanent, by design.
- **SSR response cache and writes outside the server.** In production, the
  full-page cache invalidates on writes THROUGH the server (its API/SQL
  routes). A row changed directly in the database by another process stays
  invisible on cached pages until the TTL lapses (60s default; `"responseCache"`
  in spark.json, or `false` to disable).
- **Component-import scope isolation.** A component brought in via
  `<div import="/components/foo">` has its own isolated scope — it cannot call
  ambient helpers (`refresh`/`navigate`/`api_*`) or reactive state declared in
  the page that imports it, and props are frozen at mount (not reactive
  after). A layout (`pages/_layout.html`) is the one exception — its vars
  merge into the page's own scope before compilation, so it CAN use ambient
  helpers. Pattern for components that need live ambient state: re-derive it
  from real global browser state directly (e.g. read `location.search`
  yourself) rather than expecting it to flow in.

## Docs

- [Full documentation](https://spark-html.dev/docs): concept, installation,
  components, template syntax (if/else-if/else, `<template await>`, literal
  braces), slots, reactive statements & lifecycle, JS imports, props, stores
  (incl. derived), forms, async data, JavaScript API, dev server & build,
  editor support, how it works, performance, debugging, error handling,
  prerender for SEO, and the full spark-ssr section (below).
- [spark-ssr guide](https://spark-html.dev/docs#ssr): the template is the
  backend, any data source (SQL/URL/markdown/module), ambient helpers
  (`api_create`/`api_update`/`api_delete`/`refresh`/`navigate` — ambient
  client-side helpers a hydrating page gets with zero imports; `navigate`
  intercepts same-page `<a href="?…">` clicks and swaps data via
  `history.pushState` + `refresh()` instead of a full reload), batteries
  included (auth, guards, no-JS forms, relations, jobs & mail, sitemap/robots/OpenAPI),
  fast by default (precompiled render, response cache, streaming).
- [spark-ssr full guide](https://spark-html.dev/ssr): quick start, what gets
  inferred, config, routing & layouts, data sources, a full Pinterest-style
  SQL-driven walkthrough, a markdown/module (no-database) walkthrough,
  interactive pages, auth & sessions, forms without JavaScript, the template
  as schema, background jobs & mail, performance, security, deploy.
- [Ecosystem](https://spark-html.dev/docs#ecosystem): every sibling package
  and what it does — spark-html-bun (dev server/bundler), spark-html-router,
  spark-html-theme, spark-html-head, spark-html-motion, spark-html-devtools,
  spark-html-query, spark-html-persist, spark-html-websocket, spark-prerender,
  spark-ssr, spark-html-image, spark-html-font, spark-html-manifest,
  spark-html-offline, spark-html-sri, create-spark-html-app,
  prettier-plugin-spark, spark-html-language-server.
- [Limitations](https://spark-html.dev/docs#limits): the honestly-documented
  edges of the framework — read before assuming a gap is a bug.

## Try it

- [Playground](https://spark-html.dev/playground): live in-browser editor,
  no install.
- [Tutorials](https://spark-html.dev/tutorials): guided walkthroughs.
- [Showcase](https://spark-html.dev/showcase): real apps built with Spark.

## Source

- [GitHub](https://github.com/wilkinnovo/spark-html): monorepo — all
  packages, examples (basic, jsimports, no-build, pinterest, spark-chat,
  spark-share, tabtube), and the website itself.
- [npm: spark-html](https://www.npmjs.com/package/spark-html) — the core
  runtime. Sibling packages are `spark-html-<name>` or `spark-<name>` on npm;
  see Ecosystem above for the full list.

## Optional

- [og.png](https://spark-html.dev/og.png): social preview image, not
  documentation content.
